name: redis
context: Redis in-memory data store — caching, pub/sub messaging, streams, and data structures
info: The Redis ecosystem covers in-memory data storage, caching with TTL and eviction policies, real-time pub/sub messaging across channels, durable event streaming with consumer groups, and advanced data structures including sorted sets, hashes, bitmaps, and HyperLogLog. Redis operates as a single-threaded, sub-millisecond key-value store that can also function as a message broker and stream processor. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent actually performs: caching data for fast retrieval, publishing and subscribing to real-time channels, processing ordered event streams, and leveraging specialized data structures for counting, ranking, and membership queries. The ioredis library is the primary Node.js client. Clustering, Sentinel, and replication provide high availability.
body:
# Redis Ecosystem

Redis is an in-memory data store where agents cache values, publish and subscribe to real-time message channels, process durable event streams, and leverage advanced data structures for counting, ranking, and membership. Redis delivers sub-millisecond latency and supports persistence to disk via RDB snapshots and AOF logs.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Cache data** — store and retrieve key-value pairs with TTL, eviction policies, and cache patterns (aside, through, ahead). See [[redis/caching]].
- **Publish/subscribe messages** — send and receive real-time messages across channels with pattern matching. See [[redis/pub-sub]].
- **Process event streams** — append events with XADD, read with XREAD, manage consumer groups for reliable processing. See [[redis/streams]].
- **Use data structures** — work with strings, lists, sets, sorted sets, hashes, bitmaps, and HyperLogLog for specialized workloads. See [[redis/data-structures]].
- **Configure high availability** — set up clustering, Sentinel, replication, and failover for production resilience. See [[redis/clustering]].

## Connection

All skills in this tree connect via a Redis URL:

```javascript
import Redis from "ioredis";

const redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL);
// Default: redis://localhost:6379
```

| Connection Format | Example | Use Case |
|-------------------|---------|----------|
| Standard | `redis://localhost:6379` | Local development |
| With auth | `redis://:password@host:6379` | Password-protected instances |
| TLS | `rediss://host:6380` | Cloud-managed Redis (AWS, GCP) |
| Sentinel | `redis+sentinel://host:26379` | High-availability setups |
| Cluster | `redis://node1:6379,node2:6379` | Cluster mode |

## Data Model

Redis stores all data as key-value pairs. Keys are strings. Values can be:

| Type | Commands | Use Case |
|------|----------|----------|
| String | GET, SET, INCR | Caching, counters, simple values |
| List | LPUSH, RPOP, LRANGE | Queues, recent items, timelines |
| Set | SADD, SMEMBERS, SINTER | Tags, unique items, membership |
| Sorted Set | ZADD, ZRANGE, ZRANK | Leaderboards, rate limiting, scheduling |
| Hash | HSET, HGET, HGETALL | Objects, user profiles, sessions |
| Stream | XADD, XREAD, XREADGROUP | Event logs, message queues |
| Bitmap | SETBIT, BITCOUNT | Feature flags, daily active users |
| HyperLogLog | PFADD, PFCOUNT | Cardinality estimation |

## Libraries

- **ioredis** — full-featured Redis client for Node.js with cluster and sentinel support
- **redis (node-redis)** — official Node.js client
- **redis-py** — Python Redis client
- **go-redis** — Go Redis client
- **Redlock** — distributed locking across Redis instances

sections.title: Overview
sections.body: Redis is a single-threaded, in-memory data store delivering sub-millisecond latency for reads and writes. It supports persistence via RDB snapshots and append-only files (AOF). Beyond simple key-value operations, Redis provides pub/sub messaging, durable streams with consumer groups, and specialized data structures. Use [[redis/caching]] for cache operations, [[redis/pub-sub]] for messaging, [[redis/streams]] for event processing, and [[redis/data-structures]] for advanced types.

sections.title: Connection and configuration
sections.body: Connect via `REDIS_URL` environment variable. Default is `redis://localhost:6379`. Use `rediss://` for TLS connections. ioredis handles reconnection, pipelining, and connection pooling automatically. For cluster mode, see [[redis/clustering#cluster-mode]]. For Sentinel-based HA, see [[redis/clustering#sentinel]].

sections.title: Data model
sections.body: Keys are always strings. Values can be strings, lists, sets, sorted sets, hashes, streams, bitmaps, or HyperLogLog structures. Each type has dedicated commands optimized for its access patterns. See [[redis/data-structures]] for comprehensive coverage of each type, [[redis/caching]] for string-based cache patterns, and [[redis/streams]] for stream-based event processing.
